content.problemStatement:
The kingdom of Ererraveth has NNN cities and N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 roads. A single road connects 2 cities. From any city it is possible to reach all other cities via the roads. It can be deduced that there is a unique path between any 2 cities. Each city is labeled with a unique number 111 to NNN. The city where the royal palace is located is assigned number 111.

The evil prince Drach who was once banished is planning to return and claim the empire for himself. First, he will capture the royal palace. Next, he will have his revenge on the lords of the cities. To punish a city, he will simply destroy all roads that connect it to its neighbouring cities, and the city will slowly starve.

However, there are KKK cities whose lords have supported Drach in the past. Let's call these **good cities**. Drach will ensure that all good cities remain connected via roads to his royal palace. Thus any city which is not good but lies on the path from the royal palace to a good city will be spared. But if the lord of any good city is removed from his post by the time Drach is back, he will no longer consider it a good city.

Let GGG be the set of potentially good cities with ‚à£G‚à£=K|G|=K‚à£G‚à£=K, and G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤ be a subset of GGG. Drach wants to know the number of roads he will have to destroy if G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤ be the set of good cities, i.e. if all cities in G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤ remain connected to his palace in city 111 and all cities that are not essential for the above are punished. You will need to calculate the bitwise XOR-sum of the results for all the 2K2^K2K possible subsets G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤.

###Input:


The first line contains NNN, the number of cities.
N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines follow. Each line contains two integers uuu and vvv denoting that city uuu and vvv are connected via a road.
The next line contains KKK, the number of potentially good cities
The next line contains the KKK potentially good cities G1,G2,...GKG_1, G_2, ... G_KG1‚Äã,G2‚Äã,...GK‚Äã.
###Output:


Output in a single line the XOR-sum of the number of roads Drach needs to destroy for each subset G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤ of GGG if G‚Ä≤G'G‚Ä≤ is considered the set of good cities.
###Constraints


1‚â§N‚â§1051 \leq N \leq 10^51‚â§N‚â§105
1‚â§K‚â§min‚Å°(27,N‚àí1)1 \leq K \leq \min(27, N - 1)1‚â§K‚â§min(27,N‚àí1)
2‚â§Gi‚â§N2 \leq G_i \leq N2‚â§Gi‚Äã‚â§N
All GiG_iGi‚Äã are distinct
###Sample Input:
7
1 2
1 3
1 4
2 5
3 6
3 7
2
2 7

###Sample Output:
4

###Explanation:


For G‚Ä≤={}G' = \{\}G‚Ä≤={}, 666 roads need to be destroyed.

For G‚Ä≤={2}G' = \{2\}G‚Ä≤={2}, 555 roads need to be destroyed.

For G‚Ä≤={7}G'= \{7\}G‚Ä≤={7}, 444 roads need to be destroyed.

For G‚Ä≤={2,7}G' = \{2, 7\}G‚Ä≤={2,7}, 333 roads need to be destroyed.

So the answer is 6‚äï5‚äï4‚äï3=46 \oplus 5 \oplus 4 \oplus 3 = 46‚äï5‚äï4‚äï3=4.
